text:
Let's call her Sarah.

Sarah was born in a small town to parents who loved music. They would play instruments at home, and Sarah felt the beat of "La Vie en Rose" pulsing through her bloodstream from a very young age. She'd listen to Bach while her mother rocked in prenatal gymes at six months, learning the complex harmonies of Mozart when she was an infant, and danced to the R&B anthems of the late '90s while riding pony rides around the backyard.

Sarah attended the local high school, where among her classes were those of the best drummers, keyboardists, and guitarists. She learned piano and drumsticks from an early age, and picked up on music theory with an aptitude that made her mentors shake their heads in wonder.

She skipped the typical college classes, focusing instead on music composition, orchestration, and production. With an impressive command of percussion and an ear that could spot a melody miles away, she dazzled in bands during
